info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Time Layout Sets/Web Clock Layouts
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

parameters.name: layoutSetId
parameters.description: The value of this parameter could be a hash of the key that is used to uniquely identify the resource item. The client should not generate the hash key value.  Instead, the client should query on the collection resource with a filter to navigate to a specific resource item. For example: products?q=InventoryItemId=<value1>

schema.description: Unique identifier for the layout set.

parameters.name: webClockLayoutId
parameters.description: The value of this parameter could be a hash of the key that is used to uniquely identify the resource item. The client should not generate the hash key value.  Instead, the client should query on the collection resource with a filter to navigate to a specific resource item. For example: products?q=InventoryItemId=<value1>

schema.description: Unique identifier for the web clock layout.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

buttons.description: The buttons resource provides the button details using the web clock layout identifier.

get.summary: Get all web clock layouts for a time layout set

Instead, the client should query on the collection resource with a filter to navigate to a specific resource item. For example: products?q=InventoryItemId=<value1>

schema.description: Unique identifier for the layout set.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

You can use these queryable attributes to filter this collection resource using the q query parameter:
  • allowChangeRequestForClockTimeFlag; boolean; Indicates whether a change can be requested for a time reported on Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.
  • allowManualEntryFlag; boolean; Indicates whether a manual entry for a missing clock in is enabled for Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.
  • allowedTimeDifference; number; Difference allowed between the device time and the server time, in seconds.
  • buttonLogicEnabledFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the button logic is enabled for Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.
  • clockToUse; string; Clock to use on Web Clock, either device or server.
  • displayTimeZoneFlag; boolean; Indicates whether to show time zone details on Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.
  • hideSecondsFlag; boolean; Indicates whether to hide seconds on Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.
  • restartSequenceFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the button sequence can be restarted on Web Clock after the last button is clicked. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.
  • showDailyTimeEventsFlag; boolean; Indicates whether to show daily time events. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.
  • timeZoneEffectiveEndDate; string; Date the time zone override ends.
  • webClockLayoutId; integer; Unique identifier for the web clock layout.
  • webClockTimeZoneId; string; Time zone for time on Web Clock.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified fields.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables

  • PrimaryKey Finds the web clock layout using the specified primary key criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • webClockLayoutId; integer; Unique identifier for the web clock layout.

x-adf-finder-formats.name: PrimaryKey
x-adf-finder-formats.description: Finds the web clock layout using the specified primary key criteria.

webClockLayoutId.description: Unique identifier for the web clock layout.

The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

allowChangeRequestForClockTimeFlag.description: Indicates whether a change can be requested for a time reported on Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

allowManualEntryFlag.description: Indicates whether a manual entry for a missing clock in is enabled for Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

allowedTimeDifference.description: Difference allowed between the device time and the server time, in seconds.

buttonLogicEnabledFlag.description: Indicates whether the button logic is enabled for Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

buttons.title: Buttons
buttons.description: The buttons resource provides the button details using the web clock layout identifier.

clockToUse.description: Clock to use on Web Clock, either device or server.

displayTimeZoneFlag.description: Indicates whether to show time zone details on Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

hideSecondsFlag.description: Indicates whether to hide seconds on Web Clock. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

restartSequenceFlag.description: Indicates whether the button sequence can be restarted on Web Clock after the last button is clicked. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

showDailyTimeEventsFlag.description: Indicates whether to show daily time events. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

timeZoneEffectiveEndDate.description: Date the time zone override ends.

webClockLayoutId.description: Unique identifier for the web clock layout.

webClockTimeZoneId.description: Time zone for time on Web Clock.

buttonId.description: Unique identifier for the web clock button.

buttonLabel.description: Label to show on the web clock button.

iconName.description: Icon to show on the web clock button.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

sequence.description: Unique identifier for the sequence of the web clock button.

timeAttributes.title: Time Attributes
timeAttributes.description: The timeAttributes resource provides the time attributes using the web clock button identifier.

timeCardFields.title: Time Card Fields
timeCardFields.description: The timeCardFields resource provides the time card fields using the web clock button identifier.

kind.title: kind
kind.description: The kind of the related resource.

rel.title: relation
rel.description: The name of the relation to the resource instance.  Example: self.

name.title: name
name.description: The name of the link to the related resource.

href.title: hyperlink reference
href.description: The URI to the related resource.

changeIndicator.description: Change indicator or the ETag value of the resource instance.

attributeId.description: Unique identifier for the time attribute.

attributeName.description: Name of the time attribute, such as PayrollTimeType.

attributeValue.description: Value of the time attribute, such as Regular for PayrollTimeType.

attributeValueId.description: Unique identifier for the value of the time attribute.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

administratorDataSourceId.description: Unique identifier for the administrator data source.

allowChangeRequestFlag.description: Indicates whether a change can be requested for a time card field value. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

alternateNameId.description: Unique identifier for the alternate name in the multiattribute time card field.

attributeDataType.description: Data type for the attribute from the time card field, such as VARCHAR and NUMBER.

attributeFieldId.description: Unique identifier for the time card field.

bindTcfId1.description: Bind variable for the identifier of the time card field 1.

bindTcfId2.description: Bind variable for the identifier of the time card field 2.

bindTcfId3.description: Bind variable for the identifier of the time card field 3.

bindTcfId4.description: Bind variable for the identifier of the time card field 4.

bindTcfId5.description: Bind variable for the identifier of the time card field 5.

defaultValue.description: Default value for the time card field.

defaultValueDisplay.description: Default display value for the time card field.

defaultValueUOM.description: Unit of measure for the time card field default value, such as hours or units.

displayName.description: Display name for the time card field.

displaySequence.description: Unique identifier for the sequence of the time card field.

displayType.description: Display type for the time card field, such as Hidden or SmartChoiceList.

formatBasedFlag.description: Indicates whether the time card field is format based. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

formatSubType.description: Subtype for the time card field format, such as Text, Numeric digits only, or Time.

function.description: Function used to populate a time card field for a new time entry, such as based on data source first value.

functionBasedOnAnother.description: Function used to populate the time card field based on another time card field.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

maximumLength.description: Maximum length for the time card field.

maximumValue.description: Maximum value for the time card field.

minimumValue.description: Minimum value for the time card field.

requiredFlag.description: Indicates whether the time card field is required. Valid values are true and false. The default value is false.

restURI.description: REST URI for the time card field.

scale.description: Scale for the time card field.

specificValue.description: Specific value applied to the time card field for a new time entry.

timeAttributeName.description: Name of the time attribute associated with the time card field.

timeCardFieldId.description: Unique identifier for the time card field.

timeCardFieldType.description: Type for the time card field, such as single or multiple attribute field.

unitOfMeasure.description: Unit of measure for the time card field, such as HOURS or UNITS.

userDataSourceId.description: Unique identifier for the user data source.

count.description: The number of resource instances returned in the current range.

hasMore.description: Indicates whether more resources are available on the server than the subset returned in the response. If the value is true, then there are more resources to retrieve from the server. The default value is false.

limit.description: The actual paging size used by the server.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

offset.description: The offset value used in the current page.

totalResults.description: The estimated row count when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.

Metadata-Context.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

REST-Framework-Version.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.
